Barbell Bench Press.description: A classic upper-body pressing exercise performed by lowering a barbell to the chest and pressing it back up while lying flat on a bench.
formCues: Keep your feet flat on the floor
Retract and depress your shoulder blades
Lower the bar to your mid-chest
Push yourself away from the bar to press

Dumbbell Goblet Squat.description: A front-loaded squat variation where a single dumbbell is held vertically in front of the chest, promoting an upright torso.
formCues: Hold the dumbbell vertically close to your chest
Keep your chest upright and core engaged
Drive your knees outward as you squat
Push through your heels to stand

Single-Arm Dumbbell Row.description: A unilateral horizontal pull where a single dumbbell is rowed toward the hip while supporting the body with the opposite hand and knee on a bench.
formCues: Keep your spine neutral and flat
Pull the dumbbell toward your hip, not your chest
Squeeze your shoulder blade at the top of the movement
Avoid rotating your torso as you pull

Recover.description: A designated rest period to allow your heart rate to recover and prepare for the next effort.
